name: How do I actually file a workers comp claim after hurting my hand at my warehouse job?
description: Report to employer, see doctor (say work-related), they file reports; insurer decides soon after.[2]

mainEntity.name: How do I actually file a workers comp claim after hurting my hand at my warehouse job?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report to employer, see doctor (say work-related), they file reports; insurer decides soon after.[2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Notify employer immediately, get medical treatment and tell the doctor it's work-related; employer and doctor file First Reports to insurer.[1][2] Insurer decides within 21 days (extendable).[2] In Utah, this starts your no-fault claim for medical and lost wages.[1] Key factors: Provide incident details; employer reports in 7 days; get Form 100 rights notice. Multiple jobs? Disclose for wage calc.[2] Practical guidance: Request report copies; don't miss follow-ups. If issues, contact Industrial Accidents Division at (801) 530-6800.[2] Filing promptly secures benefits faster.[3]
